name: Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail
name_original: El Tossal desde Lago de Montcortés — circular por el Bosque Sant Miquel
description: Hike the moderate 5.8-mile Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail, exploring a unique lake and enchanting forests in Catalonia.
region_intro: Hiking around Baix Pallars offers diverse landscapes within the Pyrenees, characterized by majestic mountains, serene lakes, and ancient cultural features. The region features significant elevation changes, with trails winding through low mountains and dramatic gorges. Hikers can explore old villages, historical bridle paths, and dry stone walls, adding a cultural dimension to the natural beauty. A prominent feature is the collection of beautiful lakes, including Lake Montcortès, providing areas for resting and swimming. Best hiking trails around Baix Pallars The most popular hiking route is El Tossal from Lago de Montcortés — circular through the Bosque Sant Miquel, a 5.7 miles (9.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 51 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views around Lake Montcortès. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hike from Gerri de la Sal — circular through the Sierra de Corrotes, a moderate 8.2 miles (13.2 km) path. This trail provides a circular journey through the Sierra de Corrotes, starting from Gerri de la Sal. Local hikers also love the Peramea Village – Lake Montcortés loop from Gerri de la Sal, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through cultural landscapes and past Lake Montcortès, often completed in about 3 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Baix Pallars is defined by mountainous terrain, beautiful lakes, and ancient cultural landscapes. The network offers options for various ability levels, with 5 easy, 8 moderate, and 2 difficult hiking trails available. The routes in Baix Pallars are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Baix Pallars's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Baix Pallars?
A: There are 15 hiking trails in Baix Pallars, catering to various skill levels. This includes 5 easy, 8 moderate, and 2 difficult routes, offering diverse experiences from gentle lakeside strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Baix Pallars?
A: Hikes in Baix Pallars feature a rich variety of terrain. You'll encounter majestic mountainous landscapes, serene lakes, and ancient cultural landscapes. Many trails wind through low mountains, dramatic gorges, and past old villages, often incorporating historical elements like old bridle paths and dry stone walls. Expect significant elevation changes on some routes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Baix Pallars?
A: Yes, Baix Pallars offers several easy hiking trails suitable for families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24316690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montcortés – Estany de Montcortès loop from Baix Pallars&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.4 km long and features minimal elevation gain, perfect for a leisurely outing with children.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Baix Pallars?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Baix Pallars are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular routes include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220229396/el-tossal-from-lago-de-montcortes-circular-through-the-bosque-sant-miquel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Tossal from Lago de Montcortés — circular through the Bosque Sant Miquel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1220161000/hike-from-gerri-de-la-sal-circular-through-the-sierra-de-corrotes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hike from Gerri de la Sal — circular through the Sierra de Corrotes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Baix Pallars?
A: Baix Pallars is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore stunning gorges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269223" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegats Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7920173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Noguera Pallaresa Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to beautiful lakes, notably Lake Montcortès, which offers opportunities for resting and swimming. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; for a dramatic natural spectacle.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Baix Pallars?
A: The hiking trails in Baix Pallars are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's tranquil natural environment, the majestic mountains, serene lakes, and the unique cultural dimension provided by ancient villages and historical paths.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views along the trails?
A: Yes, Baix Pallars is known for its spectacular panoramic viewpoints. While specific viewpoints are not detailed for every route, the region's mountainous terrain and gorges naturally offer many elevated spots with stunning vistas of the Noguera Pallaresa basin, reservoirs, and surrounding mountain ranges.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Baix Pallars?
A: Baix Pallars offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for exploring. Summer is also popular, especially for higher altitude trails, though some lower routes might be warm. Winter hiking is possible, particularly on lower elevation trails, but higher mountain areas may require specific gear due to snow.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to lakes or offer swimming opportunities?
A: Yes, Lake Montcortès is a prominent feature in Baix Pallars and several trails lead to or around it, offering areas for resting and swimming. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1396857" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peramea Village – Lake Montcortés loop from Gerri de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; is one such route that passes by the lake.

Q: Can I find challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in Baix Pallars?
A: Absolutely. Baix Pallars, being part of the Pyrenees, features significant elevation changes. There are 2 difficult hiking trails available for those seeking a challenge. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40309736" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peramea Village – View of the Pyrenees loop from la Pobleta de Bellveí&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 20.6 km route with over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region features impressive natural water formations. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2698547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Argenteria Cliffs and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is a notable highlight in the gorges around Baix Pallars, offering a dramatic sight for hikers.

Q: Is Baix Pallars a good base for exploring Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park?
A: Yes, Baix Pallars is situated near the entrance to the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park, making it an excellent gateway for exploring its stunning alpine landscapes, numerous glacial lakes, and diverse wildlife. The park offers a wide array of hiking trails and opportunities for multi-stage treks with mountain refuges.
tips: Climb the Sierra de Peracalç and the peak of El Tossal above the village of Cortscastell before descending again towards the lake.
Lake and one of the most important waterfowl reserves in the Pyrenees

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate. While the immediate circular route around Lake Montcortés is generally easy and suitable for all fitness levels, the full Komoot tour extends further, incorporating more significant elevation changes, making it a moderate hike overall.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: The shorter, easy circular route directly around Lake Montcortés is very family-friendly and suitable for beginners. However, the full Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail, which includes climbing towards El Tossal Peak, is rated moderate due to its length and elevation gain, making it more challenging for very young children or absolute beginners.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the exact trailhead is not provided, but generally, access to Estany de Montcortès is possible by car. Look for designated parking areas near the lake or in nearby villages. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain around Lake Montcortés features wide, accessible paths. As the trail extends into the surrounding areas, you'll encounter a mosaic landscape of pastures, cultivated fields, and oak groves, with sections that include ascents and descents, particularly on the way to El Tossal Peak.

faqs.question: What natural sights and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the tranquil beauty of Lake Montcortés, a unique karstic lake. The trail also leads to El Tossal Peak, offering panoramic views. You might also catch glimpses of the 'Geganta Adormida' (Sleeping Giantess) mountain and enjoy the enchanting forested landscapes that give the 'Bosc Encantat' its name.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on this hike?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. Around Lake Montcortés, you can spot aquatic birds like little grebes, coots, and great reed warblers. The broader region is home to raptors such as red kites, buzzards, Egyptian vultures, griffon vultures, and bearded vultures. Other animals like squirrels, wild boars, hares, partridges, and quails also inhabit the Pre-Pyrenean landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant colors, making them ideal. Summer is also popular, but it can be warmer, so an early start is recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but check local conditions for snow or ice, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trails around Estany de Montcortès are generally dog-friendly. However, as the lake is part of the Natura 2000 network and a protected area, it's crucial to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife, especially nesting birds and other sensitive species.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this area?
faqs.answer: No specific hiking permits or entrance fees are required for the Estany de Montcortès and Bosc Encantat Trail. However, the lake is included in Catalonia's Plan of Spaces of Natural Interest (PEIN) and is part of the Natura 2000 network, so please respect all environmental regulations and stay on marked paths to preserve its ecological value.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. You'll find sections that overlap with the TransCatalunya. Catalunya Central - Pirineus, the El Cinquè Llac (Stage 1 and 5), and various local paths like the Pista de Canals and Camí Vell de Montcortès.

faqs.question: What makes Estany de Montcortès unique?
faqs.answer: Estany de Montcortès is unique because it's one of the few non-glacial lakes in the Pyrenees, formed by karstic processes (dissolution of limestone). Its water input is primarily subterranean, contributing to its mysterious ambiance and making it a significant geological and ecological point of interest in the region.
